description:
Advances: mutagen.mutation_pipeline

Deliverable:
1. Pre-compute step: in `MutationRunner.run/1`, group sites by file. For each file, build a path-indexed map keyed by `site.id` (the content-addressed site ID) with `{line, column, original_ast}` as a tie-break for path lookup. Persist in `cfg.mutated_ast_cache` (or threaded local state).
2. New helper `apply_swap_at_path/3` in `mutation_runner.ex` replaces the per-site `Macro.prewalk/2` in `build_mutated_file_ast/2` (currently mutation_runner.ex:787-807). Given the path-indexed map and a site, look up the path and substitute the mutated AST at that path — O(depth) per site instead of O(file size).
3. Bare-literal sites (Literal mutator, ResultTuple mutator targeting bare booleans) fall back to the existing per-site `walk_bare/N` path. Document this in the code with a one-line comment.
4. New test `test/mutagen_ex/mutation_runner_batched_test.exs` with the byte-identity property: for every site, the file AST produced by the batched path matches byte-for-byte the file AST produced by the per-site `Macro.prewalk` path. Use the `:dispatch_mode` test seam from S3 OR a similar seam if you need finer granularity.
5. Revise `.spec/specs/mutation_pipeline.spec.md` to document the batched-pre-compute optimization (likely a new requirement covering the byte-identity property).

NOTE on splitting: if the path-indexed walker proves complex (Elixir has no native AST zipper), this stage MAY be split into S4a (path-collection walker), S4b (apply_swap_at_path), S4c (cfg threading + test). Default is single-agent; promote to split only if you hit a wall.

Verification:
- Tests to pass: `mix test` green; new `mutation_runner_batched_test.exs` green; existing `mutation_runner_test.exs` green
- Behaviors to demonstrate: determinism test green; byte-identity property test green (the file AST after batched application equals what the per-site prewalk would have produced)

Out of Scope (files):
- lib/mutagen_ex/beam_cache.ex (S5 owns)
- lib/mutagen_ex/test/code_server.ex (S5 owns)
- lib/mutagen_ex/ast.ex (S1 owns; do not extend)
- lib/mutagen_ex/ast_cache.ex (S2 owns)
- lib/mutagen_ex/mutators/dispatch.ex (S3 owns; do not modify dispatch table)
- The `restore/3` function and `with_restore/4` lifecycle (S5 owns)
Allowed touches:
- lib/mutagen_ex/mutation_runner.ex (build_mutated_file_ast + new helpers + cfg threading; do NOT touch restore path or with_restore wrapper)
- test/mutagen_ex/mutation_runner_batched_test.exs (new)
- .spec/specs/mutation_pipeline.spec.md
- CHANGELOG.md

Out of Scope (intent):
- Do NOT modify the restore path (still `Code.compile_quoted` — S5 changes that to load_binary).
- Do NOT modify the `with_restore/4` lifecycle shape (mutagen.decision.supervision_tree adjacent).
- Do NOT change the async_stream_nolink parallelism shape (.30 territory).
- The path-indexed map MUST be keyed by site.id, NOT by `{line, column}` alone. Duplicate-position sites would collide otherwise.

Merge gate:
- [ ] `mix compile --warnings-as-errors` green
- [ ] `mix test` green
- [ ] Determinism test green
- [ ] Batched-prewalk byte-identity test green
- [ ] `with_restore/4` definition unchanged (grep verification)
- [ ] Scope check: diff touches only Allowed globs
- [ ] Auditor APPROVE comment on the ticket

Commit: `mutagen-wrd.25.04: batched grouped-by-file prewalk (Layer B)`
title: S4: batched grouped-by-file prewalk (Layer B)
